original: Soviet military officer Lt. Col. Stanislav Petrov averts a worldwide nuclear war by judging a supposed missile attack from the US to be an error
Concorde makes its first non-stop crossing of the Atlantic (Washington, D.C. to Paris) in record-breaking time (3h33m).
Cosmonauts Titov & Strekalov are saved from exploding Soyuz T-10
Tsuruhiko Kiuchi rediscovers Comet Swift-Tuttle, the first time it has been spotted since 1862
Swiss pilot and inventor Yves Rossy becomes the first person to fly a jet engine-powered wing across the English Channel
Chris Sharmafree solos Es Pontàs in Mallorca, Spain, the world's first-ever 9a+ (5.15a) deep-water solo route
Apple Records releases The Beatles' 11th studio album "Abbey Road," their final recordings as a quartet, tops the charts in 11 countries
Canadian runner Ed Whitlock (73) becomes 1st person over 70 to complete a marathon in under 3 hours (2:54:48) at Toronto Marathon (Toronto, Ontario)
1st edition of The Adventures of Tintin published byHergéin Le journal de Tintin, continues until June 1993
Tessie Prevost (Williams), American civil rights pioneer (one of fist four 6-year old students to desegregate New Orleans schools), born in New Orleans, Louisiana (d. 2024)[1]
Kapiolani defies Pele (Hawaiian volcano goddess) & lives
Johnny Appleseed [John Chapman], American pioneer nurseryman (introduced apple trees to Pennsylvania, Ontario, Ohio, Indiana, and Illinois), born in Leominster, Massachusetts (d. 1845)
Australia II wins the America's Cup yacht race, the first non-US winner in 132 years
US space shuttle STS-26 launched
2 year experimental Biosphere 2 in Oracle Arizona begins

Robert Cade, American physician and inventor of Gatorade, born in San Antonio, Texas (d. 2007)
Prof Youngblood demonstrates artificial heart in Paris
"Staten Island" 1st icebreaker to enter San Francisco Bay
San Francisco GiantsBarry Bondsis 2nd MLB player to hit 40 HRs & steal 40 bases in a season
Francis Drakecompletes his circumnavigation of the world, sailing into Plymouth, England aboard the Golden Hind
TV sitcom "Gilligan's Island", starringBob Denveras Gilligan, debuts on CBS
Winsor McCay, American cartoonist (Little Nemo), born in Spring Lake, Michigan (d. 1934)
Barnes Wallis, English scientist, engineer and inventor (bouncing bomb), born in Ripley, Derbyshire, England (d. 1979)
Naomi Whitehead, current oldest living American and 3rd oldest living person in the world (114 years), born in Georgia
Because of forest fire in Br Columbia, blue moon appears in England
Emile Berliner patents the Gramophone

British ocean liner RMS Queen Mary is launched; "wins" Blue Riband for fastest passenger crossing of Atlantic, 1936 & 1938-52; retired in 1967, permanently moored and converted to a hotel in Long Beach, California
Run-DMC is the 1st rap group to hit the top 10 with "Raising Hell"
Sri Lanka complete 2-1 Test Cricket series win in Pakistan from 0-1
Vladimír Remek, Czech politician and cosmonaut (Soyuz 28), born in České Budějovice, Czechoslovakia
Douglas Wakiihuri, Kenyan athlete (World marathon champion 1987, Olympic silver 1988), born in Mombasa, Kenya
Typhoon Ketsana hits the Philippines, China, Vietnam, Cambodia, Laos and Thailand, causing 956 deaths and damage of $6.2 billion
TV comedy series "Beverly Hillbillies" premieres on CBS
"Oliver!" directed by Carol Reed and starring Mark Lester and Ron Moody premieres in London (Best Picture 1969)
Space Shuttle STS 79 (Atlantis 17), lands
TV sitcom "TheBrady Bunch" created by Sherwood Schwartz premieres on ABC in the US
Bep [Lambertus] van Klaveren, Dutch boxing champ (Olympic gold 1928), born in Rotterdam, Netherlands (d. 1992)
Jack LaLanne, American fitness and nutritional expert, born in San Francisco, California (d. 2011)
Marty Robbins, American country singer ("El Paso"; "Devil Woman"; "I Walk Alone"), born in Glendale, Arizona (d. 1982)

Ty Miller, American actor (The Kid-Young Riders), born in Granada Hills California

UN troops in Korean War recapture South Korean capital of Seoul

Britain & China initial agreement return Hong Kong to China in 1997
